description: Applies a comb of notch filters at 50 Hz and 60 Hz (plus harmonics up to Nyquist) to reject powerline interference, followed by a 2nd-order high-pass at 1 Hz to remove DC drift. The cleaned signal is scored with energy + peak, same as the baseline spike model, but on a dramatically cleaner signal. The notch filters use a simple 3-tap FIR approximation: y[n] = x[n] - 2*cos(2*pi*f/fs)*x[n-1] + x[n-2].
notes: Powerline rejection is critical for consumer EEG headsets without active shielding. The 3-tap FIR notch is cheap enough for real-time use. Combined 50/60 Hz rejection handles both US and EU power grids. Higher thresholds post-cleaning because the noise floor drops, so the same spike stands out more.
